jurisdiction: Andhra Pradesh urban local bodies (municipal corporations and municipalities) processing building permissions via AP DPMS / Online Building Permission System
regime: Andhra Pradesh Building Rules 2017 — G.O.Ms.No.119 MA&UD (28-03-2017) as amended; AP DPMS online processing

setbacks.note: Baseline calibration from fixtures/code_rules.json AP (<=100 m2). Table-17's exact treatment of sub-100 m2 plots (and its height-dependence) needs verification.

source.ref: AP Building Rules 2017 (G.O.Ms.No.119) Table-17 / Rule 57; fixtures/code_rules.json AP setbacks (calibrated engine baseline)

setbacks.note: DISCREPANCY flagged: the docs/region-research.md Table-17 extract shows 'remaining sides' of 1.5-2.0 m rising with permissible height for 100-200 m2 plots (1.5 m at 7 m height, 2.0 m at 12 m); the calibrated engine baseline uses rear/side 1.0 m and is kept here for compatibility. Verify against Table-17 before sanction-grade use.

source.ref: AP Building Rules 2017 (G.O.Ms.No.119) Table-17 / Rule 57; docs/region-research.md - AP setback note

setbacks.note: DISCREPANCY flagged: docs/region-research.md shows remaining sides of 2.0-3.0 m rising with height for 200-300 m2 (2.0 at 7 m, 2.5 at 12 m, 3.0 at 15 m), and its Tirupati default row reads front 1.5-2.0 / rear 2.0 / side1 1.5 / side2 1.0. The engine baseline (rear 1.5 / side 1.0) is kept for compatibility. This schema also models a single sideM whereas AP practice often differentiates side1/side2.

source.ref: AP Building Rules 2017 (G.O.Ms.No.119) Table-17 / Rule 57; docs/region-research.md - AP setback note

setbacks.note: AP rules permit redistributing setbacks (minimum 1.5 m on any one side for buildings up to 10 m, plots 300-750 m2). A 1 m peripheral green strip on all sides is required for plots above 300 m2.

source.ref: AP Building Rules 2017 (G.O.Ms.No.119) Table-17 / Rule 57; fixtures/code_rules.json AP setbacks (calibrated engine baseline)

setbacks.note: Baseline-engine extrapolation for large plots; Table-17 prescribes height-linked setbacks for taller buildings which this flat band does not capture.

source.ref: fixtures/code_rules.json AP setbacks (calibrated engine baseline); docs/region-research.md - AP section

heightByRoad.note: Practical cap of ~10 m (stilt + G+2) where the abutting road is under 12 m; Table-17 couples permissible height to both road width and the setbacks provided. Stilt parking floor (min 2.5 m clear) is excluded from height reckoning.

source.ref: AP Building Rules 2017 (G.O.Ms.No.119) Table-17; docs/region-research.md - AP caveats (max height ~10 m without a 12 m road)

heightByRoad.note: Up to ~15 m non-high-rise on roads of 12 m and wider, with the larger Table-17 setbacks. Buildings of 18 m and above are high-rise (minimum 12 m road, fire NOC, separate scrutiny) and outside this pack's scope.

source.ref: AP Building Rules 2017 (G.O.Ms.No.119) Table-17 / high-rise provisions; docs/region-research.md - AP caveats

far.note: AP retained an FAR-style ceiling of about 1.5-1.75 for small residential development. For non-high-rise plots the 2017 rules chiefly derive the buildable envelope from setbacks + permissible height, so 1.75 (matching fixtures/code_rules.json AP.FAR) is a calibrated practitioner default rather than a printed statutory cap.

source.ref: AP Building Rules 2017 (G.O.Ms.No.119) read with docs/region-research.md - AP confidence note (FAR ~1.75 derived estimate)

coverage.note: Matches fixtures/code_rules.json AP.maxGroundCoveragePct (65). DISCREPANCY flagged: docs/region-research.md Tirupati table lists ground coverage 60% - the engine baseline (65) is kept for compatibility; verify which applies for the target ULB/zone.

source.ref: fixtures/code_rules.json AP (calibrated engine baseline); docs/region-research.md - AP table (60%, derived estimate)

parking.note: One covered car space (about 2.5 x 5.0 m) plus two-wheeler space (about 1 x 2 m) within the plot for an individual dwelling; the stilt parking floor (min 2.5 m clear) is excluded from height reckoning. AP Rules Table-11 prescribes percentage-of-built-up-area parking for group housing (20% of built-up area for residential complexes in Municipal Corporation areas).

source.ref: AP Building Rules 2017 (G.O.Ms.No.119) Table-11; docs/region-research.md - AP parking row

rwh.note: Rainwater-harvesting structure mandatory for plots of 200 m2 and above, linked to the AP Water, Land and Trees Act (WALTA) 2002; typically a percolation pit per sanction conditions.

source.ref: AP WALTA 2002 read with AP Building Rules 2017 rainwater-harvesting provision

cornerPlot.note: Corner plots treat the second road-abutting side as a front setback (the matched band's frontM applies on that flank in place of sideM); a junction splay may additionally be required.

source.ref: AP Building Rules 2017 - corner plot treatment (common sanctioning practice)

roomMinimums.note: NBC / G.O.Ms.No.119 Table-5 derived room minimums come from fixtures/code_rules.json states.AP (habitable 9.5 m2 / 2.4 m, kitchen 5.0 m2, ceiling 2.75 m, ventilation ratio 0.1, stair 1.0 m).

docChecklist: Latest property tax / vacant land tax receipt
Approved layout copy / layout regularisation (LRS) proceedings, where applicable
Land-conversion (NALA) certificate if the land was agricultural
Building plans prepared and signed by a licensed technical person (LTP), uploaded on AP DPMS
Structural design / stability certificate and soil report, as applicable to height
Mortgage deed for 10% of built-up area in favour of the ULB, where applicable
Rainwater-harvesting (WALTA) undertaking for plots of 200 m2 and above
Situational NOCs where triggered: fire services (taller buildings), airport funnel, irrigation/water bodies, railway/defence proximity
notes: Advisory defaults for early design only - NOT legal advice and NOT a substitute for a sanctioned plan. Verify against the live AP DPMS engine and the current G.O.Ms.No.119 tables (as amended) for the specific plot.
A 1 m green strip along the frontage is mandatory; plots above 300 m2 additionally require a 1 m peripheral green strip on all sides (docs/region-research.md - AP setback note).

Mortgage clause: AP Building Rules 2017 carry a security-mortgage provision (10% of built-up area mortgaged to the sanctioning authority, released on occupancy certificate) - exact threshold and applicability NEED VERIFICATION against G.O.Ms.No.119 and its amendments.

instantApproval is null: AP's 2016-17 ease-of-doing-business reforms reportedly allowed registration-in-lieu-of-permission for small individual residential buildings (reported around plot <= 200 m2 and height <= 7 m), and AP DPMS offers self-certification tracks, but the exact statutory thresholds could not be pinned from available material - NOT modeled pending verification.

Discrepancy log vs docs/region-research.md: (a) coverage 65 (engine baseline) vs 60 (docs Tirupati table); (b) side/rear setbacks for 100-300 m2 - the Table-17 extract shows higher, height-linked values than the calibrated baseline (see band notes); (c) the docs Tirupati row uses asymmetric side1 1.5 / side2 1.0 while this schema models a single sideM; (d) docs AP toilet minimums (1.8 m2 / 1.2 m width) differ from the engine's roomClassification values (WC 1.1 m2 / 1.1 m) - room minimums stay with the state baseline, flagged here only.
